entities.name: NEMATODES
entities.description: Microscopic worm-like organisms that cause diseases in plants and lead to crop production losses.

entities.name: NEMATODE DISEASES
entities.description: Diseases caused by nematodes that result in significant monetary losses and reduced crop productivity.

entities.name: CROP PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of growing crops, which is negatively impacted by nematodes, pests, and poor soil conditions.

entities.name: BANANA
entities.description: A tropical crop that suffers spectacular damage from nematode diseases.

entities.name: COCONUT
entities.description: A tropical crop that suffers spectacular damage from nematode diseases.

entities.name: VEGETABLES
entities.description: Crops that suffer spectacular damage from nematode diseases.

entities.name: PULSES
entities.description: Crops grown in dryland agriculture that suffer cumulative damage from nematodes.

entities.name: OILSEEDS
entities.description: Crops grown in dryland agriculture that suffer cumulative damage from nematodes.

entities.name: DRYLAND AGRICULTURE
entities.description: Farming practiced in regions characterized by low soil moisture and nutrients.

entities.name: SOIL MOISTURE
entities.description: The water content of the soil, the lack of which reduces crop yields in drylands.

entities.name: NUTRIENTS
entities.description: Essential soil elements, the lack of which reduces crop yields in drylands.

entities.name: INSECT PESTS
entities.description: Insects that damage crops and further reduce potential yields.

entities.name: DEVELOPED NATIONS
entities.description: Regions where nematological problems have become increasingly apparent.

entities.name: DEVELOPING COUNTRIES
entities.description: Regions where the trend of nematological problems is gaining momentum.

entities.name: TROPICS
entities.description: Regions with favorable temperatures and long growing seasons for nematode activity and development.
